Confidential
Barclays: where the data points to opportunity
Peer benchmarking analysis anchored to the Simpler, Better, More Balanced strategy — identifying where focused action closes measurable gaps and drives bottom-line impact.
For discussion purposes only
March 2026
The overarching measure
Barclays trades at book value — the market isn't pricing in the improvement
At 0.97x P/BV, Barclays' equity is valued at roughly what it would return in liquidation — not as a going concern. Meanwhile Lloyds, a simpler bank with lower ROE, commands a 44% premium. The gap represents the market's scepticism that returns are sustainable.
£33.6bn
Market cap upside to reach Lloyds' 1.4x P/BV
£7.8bn
Value created per 0.1x P/BV improvement
0% sell
Analyst consensus: 75% Buy, 25% Hold
What the peer data reveals — mapped to the bank's own strategic framework
Simpler
The cost-to-income gap is quantifiable and the arithmetic to close it is tighter than it appears. Three specific areas where peers outperform.
Better
Capital productivity, segment returns, and the untapped Premier/Wealth opportunity. Where improved performance closes the valuation discount.
More balanced
UK lending growth, fee income diversification, and the structural hedge. Building the earnings quality that sustains a higher multiple.
Simpler
Cost-to-income ratio
The cost gap is quantifiable — and the plan leaves almost no margin for error
The arithmetic
Reaching 52% C/I on £29.1bn income requires a cost base of c.£15.1bn — roughly £2.3bn below today.
Planned efficiency savings
c.£2.0bn (2026–28)
Offsetting headwinds
c.£1.5bn acquisition costs + c.£0.8bn investment
Each 1 point of C/I = c.£290m pre-tax. Any initiative that accelerates savings or reduces the investment-to-run-rate conversion time creates direct upside.
Simpler
Employee productivity
75% of employees sit in support functions — AI savings are real but haven't yet reached scale
Operating income per employee (GBP '000, FY2025)
93k
Employees — 75% in support functions (~70k)
26%
Productivity gap vs. JPMorgan (£313k vs. £424k)
What Barclays is doing
90% Copilot adoption. 1m+ hours saved FY25. 19k developers on GitLab Duo with 15% productivity uplift. >250 AI tools deployed.
The question
1m hours saved at c.£55/hr = c.£55m. Meaningful, but a fraction of the c.£580m that a 2-point staff/income ratio improvement would deliver. Are AI savings converting to structural cost reduction — or absorbing growth?
Simpler
Non-staff costs
Staff costs are improving, but SG&A has nearly doubled in two years — the simplification dividend hasn't arrived yet
Barclays cost composition (% of income)
SG&A trend
SG&A grew from £1.9bn to £3.1bn (29% CAGR) — driven by investment and acquisition integration. This offset the staff cost improvement.
Peer benchmarks (non-staff / income)
HSBC
18.5%
Crédit Agricole
19.7%
JPMorgan
23.0%
Barclays
23.5%
Converging to 21.5% = c.£580m pre-tax. The 80+ to <40 application rationalisation and systems consolidation are the key levers — but the payoff needs to accelerate.
Better
Segment returns, capital productivity, and the wealth opportunity the data says is hiding in plain sight
Barclays' pre-tax ROE of 12.5% ranks 9th of 13 peers. The Investment Bank is improving but mid-table on capital efficiency. The Private Bank and Wealth Management segment runs the highest cost-to-income ratio of any division — yet sits on the largest untapped revenue opportunity.
Better
Investment Bank
IB returns are improving — but the path to >13% RoTE depends on extracting more income from the same capital base
6.6%
Income / avg. RWAs (2025, up from 5.5%)
>7%
2028 target
£3.5bn
Financing income (up from £2.2bn in 2021)
Return on RWAs (FY2025)
Where the capital efficiency gains come from
Risk systems: 11 → 3
Single view of risk across Prime, Fixed Income, and Structured Financing enables better netting, more efficient capital use
Electronification advancing
US IG credit: 62%→79%. Cash equity: 73%→77%. Each point reduces per-trade cost and improves scalability
Top client penetration: 30 → 62 → target 70
Of top 100 Markets clients with Top 5 rank — growing wallet without growing RWAs proportionally
Improving income/RWA from 6.6% to >7% on c.£196bn of IB RWAs = c.£780m additional income with no additional capital deployed.
Better
Private Bank and Wealth Management
The worst-performing segment by cost is also the largest untapped revenue opportunity
73.4%
Cost-to-income ratio — highest of any Barclays segment, worsening from 66.0% in 2023
4.8%
Share of group operating income
PBWM cost-to-income trend
The revenue opportunity hiding in the data
1.1m
Mass affluent customers in UK
50%
Not yet Premier — 1.6x higher deposits when they are
400k
Customers identified for new advice proposition
+169%
Direct Investing client growth (24k→65k, 96% existing BUK)
The 400k advice opportunity is 6x the current client base. At scale, a 20% income uplift at flat cost moves C/I from 73% to ~61% — but only if the digital advice platform is built for scale from day one.
Better
Barclays UK vs. Lloyds
The domestic peer shows what's achievable — and where BUK's £880m efficiency prize sits
Metric (FY2025) Barclays Lloyds
Staff cost / income 36.5% 23.7%
Income per employee £313k £327k
Pre-tax ROE 12.5% 14.2%
Loan loss / NII 15.7% 6.0%
P/BV multiple 0.97x 1.39x
Lloyds — a simpler, domestic-only bank — achieves lower staff costs relative to income and comparable income per employee despite having no investment bank revenue. This shows BUK's "Mid 40s%" target is achievable.
c.£880m
Cost reduction implied by moving BUK C/I from 56% to Mid 40s% on c.£8.8bn of income. This is ~40% of the entire group efficiency target — concentrated in one division.
The levers
Digital interaction shift: 73% → 80% target (each point = cost-per-serve reduction)
Mobile app relaunch in 2026 — platform for channel migration
Service model redesign, branch footprint optimisation, tech rationalisation
Tesco Bank integration cost normalisation (c.£0.2bn currently in BUK)
More balanced
Growth and diversification — the earnings quality that sustains a re-rating
UK lending needs to grow faster than GDP. Fee income as a share of the balance sheet lags peers by a factor of two. The structural hedge provides a mechanical tailwind — but sustainable re-rating requires demonstrating that income growth is diversified, capital-efficient, and repeatable.
More balanced
Fee income diversification
Barclays extracts half the fee income per unit of balance sheet that JPMorgan does
Non-interest income / total assets (FY2025)
Three fee engines to build on
#1
GBP clearer — Transaction Banking
iPortal consolidation (5→1 channel). Layer FX, liquidity, trade finance onto clearing franchise.
#2
US ABS franchise — Best Egg
Originate → securitise → distribute. Fee income at each stage with limited balance sheet.
New
Premier Wealth Management
65k Direct Investing clients → advice relationships → AUM-based recurring fee income.
Fee income at 5% CAGR = c.£0.9bn incremental by 2028, retaining 40–50% post-cost = c.£350–450m incremental EBT.
Summary
Nine data-anchored opportunities with quantified impact
# Pillar Barclays today Peer benchmark Action required Est. impact
1 Simpler C/I: 59.9% JPM 53%, HSBC 45% Accelerate beyond £2bn plan £290m per 1pt
2 Simpler £313k / employee JPM £424k (+36%) Support function automation £580m for 2pts
3 Simpler Non-staff: 23.5% (rising) HSBC 18.5% SG&A discipline; tech payoff £580m for 2pts
4 Better IB income/RWA: 6.6% Target >7% Risk consolidation; e-trading £780m income
5 Better PBWM C/I: 73.4% Target High 60s% Scalable digital advice C/I → ~61%
6 Better BUK C/I: 56.1% Target Mid 40s% Service model; digital shift c.£880m
7 Balanced UK lending: 5% growth Target >5% CAGR Multi-brand platform; credit £180–240m NII
8 Balanced Fee income/assets: 1.0% JPM 2.0% TxB, Wealth, ABS build c.£0.9bn by '28
9 All P/BV: 0.97x Lloyds 1.4x Deliver plan credibly £7.8bn per 0.1x
Each opportunity is anchored to peer-observable data and maps directly to Barclays' stated Simpler, Better, More Balanced strategy. Impact estimates are based on FY2025 reported financials.
The data says the plan is credible but tight. Acceleration creates disproportionate value.
Simpler
The £2.3bn cost challenge has £2.0bn of planned savings and nearly zero margin for slippage. Any acceleration compounds directly to RoTE.
Better
PBWM is the segment where technology investment has the clearest ROI case — 400k customers waiting for a proposition that doesn't yet exist at scale.
More balanced
The fee income gap to peers is the clearest signal of where earnings quality needs to improve to sustain a higher valuation multiple.
The prize: each 0.1x P/BV improvement = £7.8bn of shareholder value.
